name: AFASA — Anti-Financial Account Scamming Act (RA 12010)
summary: Criminalizes financial account scamming, enables 30-day admin freezes without court order, and establishes shared responsibility framework for unauthorized transactions.
requirements: Criminal liability for mule accounts — up to 20 years imprisonment
Financial institutions must deploy fraud management systems
30-day administrative freeze authority without court order for suspected fraud accounts
Reimbursement obligations for unauthorized transactions where FI negligence is proven
Mandatory suspicious transaction reporting to AMLC within 24 hours
Telco cooperation requirements for SIM-linked fraud investigations
penalties: Criminal penalties up to 20 years imprisonment for mule accounts and financial scamming. Financial institutions face shared liability for losses if FMS requirements are not met.

sasCapabilityMapping.specificFeature: End-to-end real-time fraud detection with adaptive ML models
Network analytics and behavioral profiling for mule identification
Automated STR generation and AMLC filing
Real-time freeze/unfreeze decision rules with audit trail

competitivePositioning: AFASA's shared liability framework makes FMS investment non-optional. SAS provides the only integrated fraud + AML platform that covers both the detection (Fraud Management) and reporting (AML) requirements in a single vendor relationship.
talkingPoints: AFASA is already in effect — non-compliance exposes institutions to shared liability NOW
Mule account detection requires network analytics — SAS AML has this built-in
30-day freeze authority means institutions need real-time decisioning capabilities
Combined fraud + AML platform reduces total cost vs. separate point solutions
SAS supports the full AFASA lifecycle: detect → freeze → investigate → report
